A) Argument and evidence checking (R1.2, R1.3), B) Novelty/‘first’ claim and scholarship positioning vs prior work (R1.4, R1.7), C) Sentence-level writing polish (R1.6), D) Section-level structure and flow (R1.5), or E) Reframing the core thesis/contribution (R1.1)? You can choose one or multiple.",
  "rationale": {
    "RX.1": "I will surface uncertainties and limits based on the brief excerpt and unspecified experimental details once the focus is defined.",
    "RX.2": "I will guard against endorsing strong claims (e.g., ‘first’ or broad generalization across languages) beyond what the text supports.",

parsed.clarification_question: What should “check this” prioritize? A) Argument and evidence checking (R1.2, R1.3), B) Novelty/‘first’ claim and scholarship positioning vs prior work (R1.4, R1.7), C) Sentence-level writing polish (R1.6), D) Section-level structure and flow (R1.5), or E) Reframing the core thesis/contribution (R1.1)? You can choose one or multiple.

rationale.RX.1: I will surface uncertainties and limits based on the brief excerpt and unspecified experimental details once the focus is defined.
rationale.RX.2: I will guard against endorsing strong claims (e.g., ‘first’ or broad generalization across languages) beyond what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: I will keep any eventual checking tightly scoped to the selected responsibility areas and avoid expanding tasks implicitly.
rationale.RX.4: I will avoid judgments that could unfairly misrepresent prior work or mislead readers about performance or novelty.
rationale.RX.5: I will tie any assessments to specific passages in the provided artifact (e.g., datasets, baselines, tables) for traceability.
